claimText: Under Model Rules 2025, felling trees on agricultural land requires a Felling Permit for more than 10 trees, and a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for 10 or fewer trees.
evidenceExcerpt: Model Rules 2025 require Felling Permits for agricultural lands with more than 10 trees, and NOCs for 10 or fewer trees.
examUse: Statement-based question on environmental regulations and agricultural land management.
distractorHooks: Stating that all tree felling requires a Felling Permit regardless of number

claimText: The felling of trees on agricultural land under Model Rules 2025 is overseen by State Level Committees, requiring applicants to register in the National Timber Management System.
evidenceExcerpt: The Government issued Model Rules 2025 for felling trees on agricultural land, overseen by State Level Committees. Applicants register in the National Timber Management System.
examUse: Institution-feature question identifying the regulatory bodies and portals involved in timber management.
distractorHooks: Oversight by Central Level Committees or the National Green Tribunal
Registration in a State Timber Management System instead of National

claimText: Agroforestry occupies approximately 8 percent of India's geographical land area.
evidenceExcerpt: Agroforestry occupies approximately 8% of India's geographical land area.
examUse: Statement-based question on Indian agriculture and land use statistics.
distractorHooks: Exaggerated coverage such as 20 percent or 30 percent

claimText: In agroforestry systems, Agrisilviculture combines agricultural crops with tree crops, whereas Silvi-pasture integrates livestock, forage, and trees.
evidenceExcerpt: Agroforestry systems include Agrisilviculture (combines crops with tree crops) and Silvi-pasture (integrates livestock, forage, and trees).
examUse: Correct-pair or match-the-following question on types of agroforestry systems.
distractorHooks: Defining Agrisilviculture as the integration of livestock

claimText: India's pledge under the Bonn Challenge included restoring 13 million hectares of degraded and deforested land by the year 2020.
evidenceExcerpt: India joined the Bonn Challenge to restore 13 million hectares of degraded and deforested land by 2020.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's international environmental commitments and land restoration targets.
distractorHooks: Target year of 2030 for the 13 million hectares pledge
Target of restoring 33 million hectares by 2020
